commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: So the prophets called him a burning fire, because in those three points we intensely the majesty of the Godhead. Since to sanctify is of the Godhead, to illuminate is the property of fire and light, and the Godhead is frequently pointed out or seen in the appearance of fire: “For our God is a consuming fire,” as Moses said. .

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Fire. God often appeared in the midst of fire. All the land shall be devoured by the fire of his jealousy, Sophonias i. 18., and iii. 8. (Calmet)
By these expressions, we are exhorted not to do any thing which would excite the indignation of our true lover, nor ever be unfaithful to him. (Haydock)
The pagans thought that fire was the fittest symbol of the divinity. (Porphyr. de Abstin.)

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: If God is fire, he is fire in order to drive out the cold of the devil.

commentaries.author: John of Damascus
commentaries.content: And the Holy Spirit descended upon the holy apostles in the form of fire, because he is God, and “God is a consuming fire.”

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
